verses.latin_vulgate: Hæc dicit Dominus ad me : Vade, et posside tibi lumbare lineum, et pones illud super lumbos tuos, et in aquam non inferes illud.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כֹּה־אָמַ֨ר יְהֹוָ֜ה אֵלַ֗י הָל֞וֹךְ וְקָנִ֤יתָ לְּךָ֙ אֵז֣וֹר פִּשְׁתִּ֔ים וְשַׂמְתּ֖וֹ עַל־מׇתְנֶ֑יךָ וּבַמַּ֖יִם לֹ֥א תְבִאֵֽהוּ׃
verses.king_james: Thus saith the Lord unto me, Go and get thee a linen girdle, and put it upon thy loins, and put it not in water.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et possedi lumbare juxta verbum Domini, et posui circa lumbos meos.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וָאֶקְנֶ֥ה אֶת־הָאֵז֖וֹר כִּדְבַ֣ר יְהֹוָ֑ה וָאָשִׂ֖ם עַל־מׇתְנָֽי׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: So I got a girdle according to the word of the Lord, and put it on my loins.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et factus est sermo Domini ad me secundo, dicens :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיְהִ֧י דְבַר־יְהֹוָ֛ה אֵלַ֖י שֵׁנִ֥ית לֵאמֹֽר׃
verses.king_james: And the word of the Lord came unto me the second time, saying,

verses.latin_vulgate: Tolle lumbare quod possedisti, quod est circa lumbos tuos : et surgens vade ad Euphraten, et absconde ibi illud in foramine petræ.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: קַ֧ח אֶת־הָאֵז֛וֹר אֲשֶׁ֥ר קָנִ֖יתָ אֲשֶׁ֣ר עַל־מׇתְנֶ֑יךָ וְקוּם֙ לֵ֣ךְ פְּרָ֔תָה וְטׇמְנֵ֥הוּ שָׁ֖ם בִּנְקִ֥יק הַסָּֽלַע׃
verses.king_james: Take the girdle that thou hast got, which is upon thy loins, and arise, go to Euphrates, and hide it there in a hole of the rock.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et abii, et abscondi illud in Euphrate, sicut præceperat mihi Dominus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וָאֵלֵ֕ךְ וָאֶטְמְנֵ֖הוּ בִּפְרָ֑ת כַּאֲשֶׁ֛ר צִוָּ֥ה יְהֹוָ֖ה אוֹתִֽי׃
verses.king_james: So I went, and hid it by Euphrates, as the Lord commanded me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et factum est post dies plurimos, dixit Dominus ad me : Surge, vade ad Euphraten, et tolle inde lumbare quod præcepi tibi ut absconderes illud ibi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיְהִ֕י מִקֵּ֖ץ יָמִ֣ים רַבִּ֑ים וַיֹּ֨אמֶר יְהֹוָ֜ה אֵלַ֗י ק֚וּם לֵ֣ךְ פְּרָ֔תָה וְקַ֤ח מִשָּׁם֙ אֶת־הָ֣אֵז֔וֹר אֲשֶׁ֥ר צִוִּיתִ֖יךָ לְטׇמְנוֹ־שָֽׁם׃
verses.king_james: And it came to pass after many days, that the Lord said unto me, Arise, go to Euphrates, and take the girdle from thence, which I commanded thee to hide there.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et abii ad Euphraten, et fodi, et tuli lumbare de loco ubi absconderam illud : et ecce computruerat lumbare, ita ut nulli usui aptum esset.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וָאֵלֵ֣ךְ פְּרָ֔תָה וָאֶחְפֹּ֗ר וָֽאֶקַּח֙ אֶת־הָ֣אֵז֔וֹר מִן־הַמָּק֖וֹם אֲשֶׁר־טְמַנְתִּ֣יו שָׁ֑מָּה וְהִנֵּה֙ נִשְׁחַ֣ת הָאֵז֔וֹר לֹ֥א יִצְלַ֖ח לַכֹּֽל׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: Then I went to Euphrates, and digged, and took the girdle from the place where I had hid it: and, behold, the girdle was marred, it was profitable for nothing.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et factum est verbum Domini ad me, dicens :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיְהִ֥י דְבַר־יְהֹוָ֖ה אֵלַ֥י לֵאמֹֽר׃
verses.king_james: Then the word of the Lord came unto me, saying,

verses.latin_vulgate: Hæc dicit Dominus :  Sic putrescere faciam superbiam Juda,  et superbiam Jerusalem multam :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כֹּ֖ה אָמַ֣ר יְהֹוָ֑ה כָּ֠כָה אַשְׁחִ֞ית אֶת־גְּא֧וֹן יְהוּדָ֛ה וְאֶת־גְּא֥וֹן יְרוּשָׁלַ֖͏ִם הָרָֽב׃
verses.king_james: Thus saith the Lord, After this manner will I mar the pride of Judah, and the great pride of Jerusalem.

verses.latin_vulgate: populum istum pessimum qui nolunt audire verba mea,  et ambulant in pravitate cordis sui,  abieruntque post deos alienos ut servirent eis et adorarent eos :  et erunt sicut lumbare istud,  quod nulli usui aptum est.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הָעָם֩ הַזֶּ֨ה הָרָ֜ע הַֽמֵּאֲנִ֣ים ׀ לִשְׁמ֣וֹעַ אֶת־דְּבָרַ֗י הַהֹֽלְכִים֙ בִּשְׁרִר֣וּת לִבָּ֔ם וַיֵּלְכ֗וּ אַֽחֲרֵי֙ אֱלֹהִ֣ים אֲחֵרִ֔ים לְעׇבְדָ֖ם וּלְהִשְׁתַּחֲוֺ֣ת לָהֶ֑ם וִיהִי֙ כָּאֵז֣וֹר הַזֶּ֔ה אֲשֶׁ֥ר לֹֽא־יִצְלַ֖ח לַכֹּֽל׃ {ס}
verses.king_james: This evil people, which refuse to hear my words, which walk in the imagination of their heart, and walk after other gods, to serve them, and to worship them, shall even be as this girdle, which is good for nothing.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sicut enim adhæret lumbare ad lumbos viri,  sic agglutinavi mihi omnem domum Israël,  et omnem domum Juda, dicit Dominus,  ut essent mihi in populum,  et in nomen, et in laudem, et in gloriam :  et non audierunt.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֡י כַּאֲשֶׁר֩ יִדְבַּ֨ק הָאֵז֜וֹר אֶל־מׇתְנֵי־אִ֗ישׁ כֵּ֣ן הִדְבַּ֣קְתִּי אֵ֠לַ֠י אֶת־כׇּל־בֵּ֨ית יִשְׂרָאֵ֜ל וְאֶת־כׇּל־בֵּ֤ית יְהוּדָה֙ נְאֻם־יְהֹוָ֔ה לִֽהְי֥וֹת לִי֙ לְעָ֔ם וּלְשֵׁ֥ם וְלִתְהִלָּ֖ה וּלְתִפְאָ֑רֶת וְלֹ֖א שָׁמֵֽעוּ׃
verses.king_james: For as the girdle cleaveth to the loins of a man, so have I caused to cleave unto me the whole house of Israel and the whole house of Judah, saith the Lord; that they might be unto me for a people, and for a name, and for a praise, and for a glory: but they would not hear.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dices ergo ad eos sermonem istum :   Hæc dicit Dominus Deus Israël :  Omnis laguncula implebitur vino. Et dicent ad te :  Numquid ignoramus quia omnis laguncula implebitur vino ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְאָמַרְתָּ֨ אֲלֵיהֶ֜ם אֶת־הַדָּבָ֣ר הַזֶּ֗ה {ס} כֹּֽה־אָמַ֤ר יְהֹוָה֙ אֱלֹהֵ֣י יִשְׂרָאֵ֔ל כׇּל־נֵ֖בֶל יִמָּ֣לֵא יָ֑יִן וְאָמְר֣וּ אֵלֶ֔יךָ הֲיָדֹ֙עַ֙ לֹ֣א נֵדַ֔ע כִּ֥י כׇל־נֵ֖בֶל יִמָּ֥לֵא יָֽיִן׃
verses.king_james: Therefore thou shalt speak unto them this word; Thus saith the Lord God of Israel, Every bottle shall be filled with wine: and they shall say unto thee, Do we not certainly know that every bottle shall be filled with wine?

verses.latin_vulgate: Et dices ad eos :  Hæc dicit Dominus :  Ecce ego implebo omnes habitatores terræ hujus,  et reges qui sedent de stirpe David super thronum ejus,  et sacerdotes, et prophetas,  et omnes habitatores Jerusalem, ebrietate.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְאָמַרְתָּ֨ אֲלֵיהֶ֜ם כֹּֽה־אָמַ֣ר יְהֹוָ֗ה הִנְנִ֣י מְמַלֵּ֣א אֶת־כׇּל־יֹשְׁבֵ֣י הָאָ֪רֶץ הַזֹּ֟את וְאֶת־הַמְּלָכִ֣ים הַיֹּשְׁבִים֩ לְדָוִ֨ד עַל־כִּסְא֜וֹ וְאֶת־הַכֹּהֲנִ֣ים וְאֶת־הַנְּבִאִ֗ים וְאֵ֛ת כׇּל־יֹשְׁבֵ֥י יְרוּשָׁלָ֖͏ִם שִׁכָּרֽוֹן׃
verses.king_james: Then shalt thou say unto them, Thus saith the Lord, Behold, I will fill all the inhabitants of this land, even the kings that sit upon David’s throne, and the priests, and the prophets, and all the inhabitants of Jerusalem, with drunkenness.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et dispergam eos virum a fratre suo,  et patres et filios pariter, ait Dominus.  Non parcam, et non concedam :  neque miserebor, ut non disperdam eos.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְנִפַּצְתִּים֩ אִ֨ישׁ אֶל־אָחִ֜יו וְהָאָב֧וֹת וְהַבָּנִ֛ים יַחְדָּ֖ו נְאֻם־יְהֹוָ֑ה לֹֽא־אֶחְמ֧וֹל וְלֹא־אָח֛וּס וְלֹ֥א אֲרַחֵ֖ם מֵהַשְׁחִיתָֽם׃
verses.king_james: And I will dash them one against another, even the fathers and the sons together, saith the Lord: I will not pity, nor spare, nor have mercy, but destroy them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Audite, et auribus percipite :  nolite elevari, quia Dominus locutus est.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שִׁמְע֥וּ וְהַאֲזִ֖ינוּ אַל־תִּגְבָּ֑הוּ כִּ֥י יְהֹוָ֖ה דִּבֵּֽר׃
verses.king_james: Hear ye, and give ear; be not proud: for the Lord hath spoken.

verses.latin_vulgate: Date Domino Deo vestro gloriam  antequam contenebrescat,  et antequam offendant pedes vestri ad montes caliginosos :  exspectabitis lucem,  et ponet eam in umbram mortis, et in caliginem.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: תְּנוּ֩ לַיהֹוָ֨ה אֱלֹהֵיכֶ֤ם כָּבוֹד֙ בְּטֶ֣רֶם יַחְשִׁ֔ךְ וּבְטֶ֛רֶם יִֽתְנַגְּפ֥וּ רַגְלֵיכֶ֖ם עַל־הָ֣רֵי נָ֑שֶׁף וְקִוִּיתֶ֤ם לְאוֹר֙ וְשָׂמָ֣הּ לְצַלְמָ֔וֶת (ישית) [וְשִׁ֖ית] לַעֲרָפֶֽל׃
verses.king_james: Give glory to the Lord your God, before he cause darkness, and before your feet stumble upon the dark mountains, and, while ye look for light, he turn it into the shadow of death, and make it gross darkness.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quod si hoc non audieritis,  in abscondito plorabit anima mea a facie superbiæ :  plorans plorabit,  et deducet oculus meus lacrimam,  quia captus est grex Domini.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְאִם֙ לֹ֣א תִשְׁמָע֔וּהָ בְּמִסְתָּרִ֥ים תִּבְכֶּֽה־נַפְשִׁ֖י מִפְּנֵ֣י גֵוָ֑ה וְדָמֹ֨עַ תִּדְמַ֜ע וְתֵרַ֤ד עֵינִי֙ דִּמְעָ֔ה כִּ֥י נִשְׁבָּ֖ה עֵ֥דֶר יְהֹוָֽה׃ {ס}
verses.king_james: But if ye will not hear it, my soul shall weep in secret places for your pride; and mine eye shall weep sore, and run down with tears, because the Lord’s flock is carried away captive.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dic regi et dominatrici :  Humiliamini, sedete,  quoniam descendit de capite vestro corona gloriæ vestræ.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֱמֹ֥ר לַמֶּ֛לֶךְ וְלַגְּבִירָ֖ה הַשְׁפִּ֣ילוּ שֵׁ֑בוּ כִּ֤י יָרַד֙ מַרְאֲשׁ֣וֹתֵיכֶ֔ם עֲטֶ֖רֶת תִּֽפְאַרְתְּכֶֽם׃
verses.king_james: Say unto the king and to the queen, Humble yourselves, sit down: for your principalities shall come down, even the crown of your glory.

verses.latin_vulgate: Civitates austri clausæ sunt,  et non est qui aperiat :  translata est omnis Juda transmigratione perfecta.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עָרֵ֥י הַנֶּ֛גֶב סֻגְּר֖וּ וְאֵ֣ין פֹּתֵ֑חַ הׇגְלָ֧ת יְהוּדָ֛ה כֻּלָּ֖הּ הׇגְלָ֥ת שְׁלוֹמִֽים׃ {ס}
verses.king_james: The cities of the south shall be shut up, and none shall open them: Judah shall be carried away captive all of it, it shall be wholly carried away captive.

verses.latin_vulgate: Levate oculos vestros et videte,  qui venitis ab aquilone :  ubi est grex qui datus est tibi,  pecus inclytum tuum ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: (שאי) [שְׂא֤וּ] עֵֽינֵיכֶם֙ (וראי) [וּרְא֔וּ] הַבָּאִ֖ים מִצָּפ֑וֹן אַיֵּ֗ה הָעֵ֙דֶר֙ נִתַּן־לָ֔ךְ צֹ֖אן תִּפְאַרְתֵּֽךְ׃
verses.king_james: Lift up your eyes, and behold them that come from the north: where is the flock that was given thee, thy beautiful flock?

verses.latin_vulgate: Quid dices cum visitaverit te ?  tu enim docuisti eos adversum te,  et erudisti in caput tuum.  Numquid non dolores apprehendent te,  quasi mulierem parturientem ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מַה־תֹּֽאמְרִי֙ כִּֽי־יִפְקֹ֣ד עָלַ֔יִךְ וְ֠אַ֠תְּ לִמַּ֨דְתְּ אֹתָ֥ם עָלַ֛יִךְ אַלֻּפִ֖ים לְרֹ֑אשׁ הֲל֤וֹא חֲבָלִים֙ יֹֽאחֱז֔וּךְ כְּמ֖וֹ אֵ֥שֶׁת לֵדָֽה׃
verses.king_james: What wilt thou say when he shall punish thee? for thou hast taught them to be captains, and as chief over thee: shall not sorrows take thee, as a woman in travail?

verses.latin_vulgate: Quod si dixeris in corde tuo : Quare venerunt mihi hæc ?  propter multitudinem iniquitatis tuæ  revelata sunt verecundiora tua,  pollutæ sunt plantæ tuæ.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְכִ֤י תֹֽאמְרִי֙ בִּלְבָבֵ֔ךְ מַדּ֖וּעַ קְרָאֻ֣נִי אֵ֑לֶּה בְּרֹ֧ב עֲוֺנֵ֛ךְ נִגְל֥וּ שׁוּלַ֖יִךְ נֶחְמְס֥וּ עֲקֵבָֽיִךְ׃
verses.king_james: And if thou say in thine heart, Wherefore come these things upon me? For the greatness of thine iniquity are thy skirts discovered, and thy heels made bare.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si mutare potest Æthiops pellem suam,  aut pardus varietates suas,  et vos poteritis benefacere,  cum didiceritis malum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הֲיַהֲפֹ֤ךְ כּוּשִׁי֙ עוֹר֔וֹ וְנָמֵ֖ר חֲבַרְבֻּרֹתָ֑יו גַּם־אַתֶּם֙ תּוּכְל֣וּ לְהֵיטִ֔יב לִמֻּדֵ֖י הָרֵֽעַ׃
verses.king_james: Can the Ethiopian change his skin, or the leopard his spots? then may ye also do good, that are accustomed to do evil.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et disseminabo eos quasi stipulam  quæ vento raptatur in deserto.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַאֲפִיצֵ֖ם כְּקַשׁ־עוֹבֵ֑ר לְר֖וּחַ מִדְבָּֽר׃
verses.king_james: Therefore will I scatter them as the stubble that passeth away by the wind of the wilderness.

verses.latin_vulgate: Hæc sors tua,  parsque mensuræ tuæ a me, dicit Dominus,  quia oblita es mei,  et confisa es in mendacio.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: זֶ֣ה גוֹרָלֵ֧ךְ מְנָת־מִדַּ֛יִךְ מֵאִתִּ֖י נְאֻם־יְהֹוָ֑ה אֲשֶׁר֙ שָׁכַ֣חַתְּ אוֹתִ֔י וַֽתִּבְטְחִ֖י בַּשָּֽׁקֶר׃
verses.king_james: This is thy lot, the portion of thy measures from me, saith the Lord; because thou hast forgotten me, and trusted in falsehood.

verses.latin_vulgate: Unde et ego nudavi femora tua contra faciem tuam,  et apparuit ignominia tua :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְגַם־אֲנִ֛י חָשַׂ֥פְתִּי שׁוּלַ֖יִךְ עַל־פָּנָ֑יִךְ וְנִרְאָ֖ה קְלוֹנֵֽךְ׃
verses.king_james: Therefore will I discover thy skirts upon thy face, that thy shame may appear.

verses.latin_vulgate: adulteria tua, et hinnitus tuus,  scelus fornicationis tuæ :  super colles in agro vidi abominationes tuas.  Væ tibi, Jerusalem !  non mundaberis post me : usquequo adhuc ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: נִאֻפַ֤יִךְ וּמִצְהֲלוֹתַ֙יִךְ֙ זִמַּ֣ת זְנוּתֵ֔ךְ עַל־גְּבָעוֹת֙ בַּשָּׂדֶ֔ה רָאִ֖יתִי שִׁקּוּצָ֑יִךְ א֥וֹי לָךְ֙ יְר֣וּשָׁלַ֔͏ִם לֹ֣א תִטְהֲרִ֔י אַחֲרֵ֥י מָתַ֖י עֹֽד׃ {ס}
verses.king_james: I have seen thine adulteries, and thy neighings, the lewdness of thy whoredom, and thine abominations on the hills in the fields. Woe unto thee, O Jerusalem! wilt thou not be made clean? when shall it once be?
